title: 10 best herbal teas for hyperacidity
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hyperacidity are infusions made from various plants that can help alleviate the symptoms of hyperacidity. Hyperacidity occurs when there is an excess production of stomach acid, leading to discomfort, pain, and other digestive issues. Herbal teas like chamomile, ginger, licorice, and marshmallow root are some of the popular choices for treating hyperacidity. These teas work by reducing inflammation, coating the stomach lining, and promoting digestion. By drinking these teas, individuals can experience relief from symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn, bloating, and nausea. As a result, they can enjoy improved digestion, reduced discomfort, and an overall better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with hyperacidity because it contains a natural compound called chamazulene, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive tract. This soothing effect helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, allowing for better digestion and absorption of nutrients. Additionally, yarrow's ability to stimulate saliva production helps to neutralize excess acidity in the mouth and esophagus, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion symptoms associated with hyperacidity.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Yarrow tea helps neutralize excess stomach acid, reducing heartburn and bloating, by forming a physical barrier that prevents further acid production in the stomach.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Yarrow's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by hyperacidity.

Antioxidant activity: Yarrow tea's antioxidant compounds help scavenge free radicals in the body, which can contribute to oxidative stress and exacerbate conditions like hyperacidity, allowing for a more balanced digestive environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort associated with hyperacidity.
Seeds: Rich in alkaloids that help neutralize excess stomach acid and soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves, flowers, and stems for a total of one ounce per cup.
Heat one quart of water in a large pot until it reaches a rolling boil within ten minutes.
Add the one ounce of yarrow herb to the boiling water and steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large container, discarding solids after fifteen minutes.
Allow the cooled tea to infuse for at least thirty minutes before serving, resulting in a flavorful and aromatic brew.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort teas helps with hyperacidity because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive tract. The tea's bitter compounds, including sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the stomach lining, which can contribute to heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, mugwort's astringent properties help to neutralize excess stomach acid, providing relief from symptoms of hyperacidity. By promoting a healthy balance of digestive enzymes and reducing inflammation, mugwort teas can provide long-term relief from hyperacidity and related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Mugwort tea helps to neutralize excess stomach acid by constricting the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues.
Antioxidant properties: Mugwort's high antioxidant content helps to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hyperacidity and inflammation.
Carminative properties: Mugwort tea's ability to expel gas and reduce spasms in the digestive tract can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate hyperacidity symptoms.
Seeds: Contain mucilages that soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mugwort leaves from a trusted source or purchase dried form at local health food store.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried mugwort or three teaspoons of fresh leaves per eight ounce cup.
Bring filtered water to boil in a stainless steel teapot or ceramic infuser and remove from heat.
Steep mugwort in boiling water for seven to ten minutes allowing flavors and benefits to mingle.
Strain tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate cup discarding solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with hyperacidity because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and amino acids that help to neutralize excessive acid production in the stomach. The high levels of catechins present in white tea, particularly EGCG, have been shown to inhibit the growth of helicobacter pylori bacteria, which can contribute to hyperacidity. Additionally, the astringent properties of white tea help to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, further alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: White tea's high antioxidant content helps neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal white teas like peppermint, chamomile, and lemon balm contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with hyperacidity.

Digestive enzymes: Certain herbal white tea ingredients, such as ginger and licorice root, stimulate the production of digestive enzymes that help break down food more efficiently, reducing the risk of acid reflux and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as stomach pain and inflammation.
Bark: The bark contains alkaloids and tannins that help neutralize excess acid in the stomach and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality camellia sinensis leaves at dawn or late afternoon for optimal flavor.
Rinse the harvested leaves with filtered water to remove impurities and excess moisture.
Steep one teaspoon of dried white tea leaves in boiling water for three to five minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve to prevent loose particles from entering.
Serve the herbal white tea hot, garnished with lemon slices or mint sprigs if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory teas helps with hyperacidity because of its natural alkalizing properties, which help to neutralize excess stomach acid. The tea's active compounds, including inulin and saponins, work together to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. By doing so, chicory tea helps to calm down the production of excessive stomach acid, providing relief from symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and discomfort after meals.
remedy_properties: Alkalinity: Chicory tea has a natural alkaline effect on the body, which helps to neutralize excess acidity in the stomach and intestines, providing relief from symptoms like heartburn and bloating.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants present in chicory tea help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to hyperacidity, and also protect against oxidative stress caused by excessive acid production.

Soften Mucus: Chicory tea's mucilages (soft, gel-like substances) help to soften excess mucus in the digestive tract, making it easier to expel and reducing inflammation, which can further exacerbate hyperacidity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of inulin, chicory roots help neutralize stomach acidity and soothe digestive issues associated with hyperacidity.
Bark: The bark of the chicory plant contains tannins that have natural antacid properties, making it an effective ingredient in teas for alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chicory roots and cut them into small pieces.
Rinse the chicory root pieces in cold running water to remove any dirt.
Combine the chicory root with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug.
Add honey or lemon to taste and enjoy your soothing herbal tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka teas helps with hyperacidity because of its unique blend of herbs that work together to neutralize excess stomach acid. The tea's natural ingredients, such as ginger and chamomile, help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can contribute to acid buildup in the body, promoting a healthy balance of pH levels in the stomach and digestive tract.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Eureka tea's antioxidants help neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce inflammation, thus providing relief from hyperacidity symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Eureka tea can reduce the production of gastric acid and soothe the digestive tract, alleviating hyperacidity discomfort.
Carminative properties: Eureka tea's carminative properties help to expel excess gas and relieve bloating, which can exacerbate hyperacidity symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and glycosides that help neutralize excess acid in the stomach and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrus limon leaves or use dried ones measuring 2 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the harvested leaves with boiling water in a heat-resistant pitcher or teapot for 7-10 minutes steeping.
Strain the tea into cups using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove any solids and sediment.
Add honey or sugar to taste and stir well if desired to enhance flavor profile and sweetness level.
Serve hot or chilled immediately within 15-30 minutes after preparation for optimal aroma and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Key lime teas helps with hyperacidity because it contains a blend of natural herbs that work in harmony to neutralize excess stomach acid. The citrusy flavor and aroma of key lime, combined with soothing herbs like ginger and slippery elm, help to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the gut. As a result, herbal key lime teas can provide relief from symptoms such as bloating, discomfort, and heartburn associated with hyperacidity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Key lime tea's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize excess acidity by protecting the body from oxidative stress and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory agents in key lime tea, like limonoids and flavonoids, reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to hyperacidity symptoms like heartburn and bloating.

Alkalizing effects: Key lime tea's high pH level helps counterbalance excessive acidity by introducing alkaline compounds into the body, promoting a healthy balance of bodily fluids and reducing acidity-related discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the key lime plant have been traditionally used to create a soothing tea that can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn and indigestion.
Bark: The bark of the key lime plant contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation caused by hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried citrus aurantifolia leaves and 1 tablespoon of granulated sugar in a clean tea infuser.
Boil 8 ounces of fresh water for 3 minutes and then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Steep the citrus aurantifolia leaves in the hot water for 5-7 minutes or until the desired flavor is achieved.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser and discard the solids, leaving 8 ounces of herbal tea.
Add the granulated sugar to taste and stir well, then serve immediately and enjoy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with hyperacidity because it contains curcumin, a powerful compound that has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When consumed as a tea, curcumin works to neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, turmeric's natural antacid properties help to soothe the digestive system and promote a healthy balance of gut flora, further supporting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by hyperacidity, alleviating symptoms such as heartburn and bloating.

Antacid properties: Turmeric teas contain natural antacids like calcium carbonate and magnesium hydroxide that help neutralize excess stomach acid, providing quick relief from hyperacidity symptoms like burning sensation and discomfort.

Gastric mucosa protective effects: Curcumin in turmeric tea has been shown to protect the gastric mucosa (the lining of the stomach) from damage caused by excess acid production, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric's rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe digestive issues caused by hyperacidity.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are rich in calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried turmeric powder or two tablespoons of fresh turmeric root.
Heat water in a saucepan or kettle to a boil for 2-3 minutes.
Add the measured turmeric powder or fresh turmeric root to the boiling water and stir well.
Reduce heat to simmer for 5-7 minutes allowing flavors to infuse and mixture to thicken slightly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with hyperacidity because of its ability to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by excess acidity. The flavonoids present in licorice root have anti-inflammatory properties that help calm down the digestive system, while its demulcent nature forms a protective barrier against acidic digestive juices, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion symptoms. This natural remedy can help alleviate hyperacidity without resorting to harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound found in licorice root, glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.

Licoricidin: A flavonoid present in licorice root, licoricidin has been shown to have protective effects on the mucous membranes in the stomach, helping to soothe and heal ulcers and other lesions caused by hyperacidity.

Triterpenoids: Licorice root contains a group of compounds called triterpenoids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antacid properties that help reduce the production of stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.

Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including hyperacidity, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root per eight ounces water.
Heat the mixture in a saucepan over medium heat for approximately seven minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or cup.
Add one to two sweeteners like honey or sugar as desired and stir well.
Allow the tea to steep for at least three to five minutes before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus teas helps with hyperacidity because it contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes. These compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and increase mucus production, which can help neutralize excess stomach acid. Additionally, humulus lupulus teas may also stimulate the relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing acid reflux and promoting a healthier digestive environment.
remedy_properties: Xanthohumol: A flavonoid compound found in hops, xanthohumol has been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and healing ulcers caused by acid reflux.

Farnesoid X receptor (FXR) agonism: Humulus lupulus tea contains compounds that activate FXR receptors, which play a crucial role in regulating bile acid synthesis, resulting in improved gut motility and reduced symptoms of hyperacidity.

Soothing and calming effects: The flavonoids and other bioactive compounds present in humulus lupulus tea have a calming effect on the digestive tract, reducing spasms, inflammation, and acid production, thereby providing relief from heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Humulus lupulus roots have been traditionally used to reduce stomach acidity and alleviate digestive issues due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: The buds of the humulus lupulus plant contain bioactive compounds that help to neutralize excess acid in the stomach, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried hops flowers per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the hops in hot water for ten to fifteen minutes or until a desired strength achieved.
Strain the liquid using a piece of cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a cup or teapot.
Add honey and lemon to taste if desired and stir well to combine ingredients.
Allow tea to cool slightly before serving warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound teas helps with hyperacidity because it contains alkaline properties that help neutralize excess stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion. The tea's soothing properties also calm irritated mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hyperacidity. Additionally, horehound's natural antibacterial properties may help eliminate H. pylori bacteria, which can contribute to stomach ulcers and acid reflux.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Horehound tea has antacid properties that help neutralize excess stomach acid and relieve heartburn and indigestion symptoms, reducing discomfort and inflammation in the esophagus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds present in horehound tea, such as marrubilin and oleanolic acid, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity.

Gastroprotective properties: Horehound tea has been shown to possess gastroprotective properties, which means it helps protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excessive acid production, reducing the risk of ulcers and other complications associated with hyperacidity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds, horehound roots help to neutralize stomach acids and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.
Bark: The bark of the horehound plant contains mucilages that soothe and protect the digestive tract, making it an effective ingredient for teas addressing hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried horehound leaves and flowers at dawn to ensure optimal potency.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried horehound herb or three teaspoons of fresh horehound per 8 oz cup water.
Bring filtered water to a boil in a saucepan and pour it over the horehound, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a heat-resistant cup, discarding solids immediately.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature before serving and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hyperacidity with herbal teas is by consuming chamomile, marshmallow root, and licorice root teas. These teas have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity. Additionally, they promote the production of mucus in the stomach, which can protect the stomach lining from acid. Drinking these teas after meals and before bedtime can help prevent acid reflux and provide relief from hyperacidity. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperacidity that are treated with herbal teas include acid reflux, gastritis, and indigestion. Herbal teas such as chamomile, ginger, and licorice root are known for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and esophagus, and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and nausea. Peppermint tea can also aid in digestion and alleviate gas, but should be avoided if experiencing acid reflux as it can worsen symptoms. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
